validation_gates.reason: The target remains under tests/, the enforceability root; docs, contracts, schemas, registry, policy, validators, fixtures, evidence, lifecycle data, release state, and public implementation remain in their owning roots.

The target prior blob was rechecked on the authoring base; intervening main changes were unrelated to the target and inspected Atmosphere evidence. No matching task branch or duplicate document ID surfaced before mutation; the open-PR search endpoint was intermittently unavailable.

The README preserves the canonical prose explainer, thin registry index, domain and cross-domain semantic contracts, permissive schema, machine-registry placeholder, policy scaffold, validator boundary, fixture root, and test lane as distinct authority surfaces.
The README does not freeze canonical enum values, casing, umbrella-term handling, or machine-registry placement while those decisions remain open and ADR-class.

The direct child is documented as README-only; the adjacent parent-level test is recorded as a one-line PROPOSED placeholder; schema, registry, policy, validator, workflow, fixture, and collection maturity are not overstated.
The committed README has one H1, twenty-two fenced-block delimiter lines, a final newline, unique local heading anchors, and all checked internal fragment links resolve.

A temporary branch-only verification workflow measured the committed artifact as Git blob 4c8e0aa3c246eb76624eb635bff2024cb4d5f2b6, 69745 bytes, 1219 lines, and SHA-256 8e4c17b1ab909c8f594ebc3becf644cc8137189a6b606fba73f88511a995939c; the workflow was then removed.

No common credential patterns, real source payloads, private endpoints, current advisory text, protected location data, official warnings, health guidance, emergency instructions, or production trust artifacts were introduced.
This change is documentation-only; the direct lane has no established executable tests, and the adjacent named test is a placeholder.
Final repository workflows had not completed when this receipt was emitted; status must be read from the final pull-request head.
